name: investigate_search_criteria_for_product_and_category
summary: Use Admin API search endpoints plus entity-schema inspection to validate product/category Criteria queries and reduce guessing around term, filter, and field names.

steps.purpose: Discover manufacturers first so product-by-manufacturer examples have a valid manufacturer ID to use.
notes: The entity name product-manufacturer is not obvious to new users.
Typical includes payloads should expose id and name for follow-up queries.

steps.purpose: List or filter products with Criteria queries.
notes: Use filter for exact field constraints like manufacturerId.
Use term for full-text style matching when you want search semantics rather than exact field filters.
Use sort clauses to validate which product fields are actually useful for stable ordered results.

steps.purpose: List or search categories with Criteria queries.
notes: Categories often need both term and filter examples because term vs contains is easy to confuse.
Seed data assumptions matter: empty results may reflect missing fixture data rather than invalid criteria.

steps.purpose: Inspect entity schema metadata to discover field names before building Criteria filters and sorts.
notes: Field discoverability is still incomplete for searchable vs filterable vs sortable capabilities.

prerequisites: Admin API calls use a Bearer token, and short-lived tokens can interrupt exploratory work.
Product and category examples are only meaningful when the local instance has usable seed data.
commonFailureReasons: Empty results caused by sparse local seed data rather than invalid queries.
Unclear field names such as manufacturerId vs manufacturer.name when building filters.
Confusion about when to use term versus explicit filter operators like equals or contains.

recommendedExamples.pattern: filter on manufacturerId with a small includes block
term-based query with limit and minimal includes
compare term against contains filter on name

recommendedExamples.job: products sorted by name or created date
recommendedExamples.pattern: add sort clauses after verifying the field names in entity schema

diagnosticChecks: Run the same query without filters to separate data absence from bad filter design.
Compare term-based queries with explicit contains or equals filters on the same field.
Use entity schema and route examples together before assuming a field is searchable, filterable, or sortable.
